steps.title: Recognize the square pattern
steps.content:
The first and last terms are perfect squares:
$$9a^2=(3a)^2$$
$$16b^2=(4b)^2$$

steps.title: Check the middle term
steps.content:
For a square of a binomial, the middle term is twice the product of the two base terms:
$$2(3a)(4b)=24ab$$
So the polynomial factors as
$$(3a+4b)^2$$
